hero.eyebrow: Mutual aid · Pet sitting · Home sitting
hero.lede: Have your home and pets looked after by a local caretaker / pet & home sitter while you are away. Or request, and return, a small favour near you, with or without animals.
hero.lede_italic: And behind every exchange, a meeting you wouldn't have looked for.

usages.title: In-home pet sitting and local mutual aid.
usages.lede: Home sitting on one side, mutual aid between locals on the other. You choose: one, the other, or both.

owner.title: Leave with peace of mind.
owner.text: Someone from the local area watches over your home and animals. You meet them before leaving. You choose.
owner.cta: I'm looking for a caretaker / pet & home sitter →

sitter.title: Care, and discover.
sitter.text: Live in homes, with animals, in places you would never have explored otherwise. A sit is also a journey.

mutual.tag: Local mutual aid, independent of house-sitting
mutual.title: A small favour, an exchange.
mutual.text: Watering a vegetable garden, putting up a shelf, sharing a skill, visiting an isolated person. No sitting, no animals required, just locals helping each other.

urgency.title: A sudden departure?
urgency.text: Hospitalization, last-minute travel, family emergency: a local caretaker / pet & home sitter can take over in a few hours.

meeting.eyebrow: What is never written in an ad
meeting.p1: What is rarely told is what happens around it. Getting to know the local grandmother and her story because you helped her pick fruit from her garden. Exchanging with Gerardo while he gives you a small favour with DIY, and offering him a home-cooked meal in return.
meeting.p2: You don't need a pet for a connection to begin. Asking for a small favour, or offering one: it is already opening a door. Looking after a house is opening another, the same gesture, two scales.
meeting.p3: We learn, discover, and grow attached, without looking for it. Just by leaving space for what might happen.

how.title: How it works?
how.step1_text: Your dates, your animals, your home. It only takes a few minutes, your listing is online.
how.step2_text: Local sitters apply. You read profiles, reviews, and exchange. Then you meet for a coffee, a visit, before deciding.
how.step3_text: With the house-sitting agreement signed, your sitter moves in. You receive news. You return home, often with a new relationship.
how.cta_secondary: Or start with a small favour →
how.footnote: Register in 2 minutes · No credit card required

aid.eyebrow: For everyone · No subscription · No money
aid.title: Dare to ask. Dare to offer.
aid.p1: Before, there was someone local who would come by to water the garden, look after the dog, or fix a shutter. Someone to whom we would offer a slice of cake or a small favour in return.
aid.p2: This habit hasn't disappeared, we simply no longer dare to ask the first question. Guardiens is the pretext to ask it again, and along the way, live experiences that no organized tour will offer you.
aid.need_text: Your vegetable garden during the holidays. Your chickens this weekend. Walking your dog after an operation. Asking for it isn't admitting a weakness, it's placing trust.
aid.need_footer: No money, no subscription. That's the gamble.
aid.offer_title: « I can give a little of my time »
aid.offer_text: You know how to prune roses. You love dogs. You have two hours on Saturday morning. Make an offer, and live what you would never have experienced without this meeting.
aid.offer_footer: This is how a local fabric is re-woven.
aid.seen_this_week: A few exchanges seen on Guardiens this week
aid.quote: Village life hasn't disappeared. It was simply waiting for someone to dare the first question.

trust.title: Choose with confidence, one mile or a thousand away.
trust.lede: Four guideposts to leave peacefully: free for owners, a meeting before every sit, verified profiles, and a perimeter you set yourself.
trust.p1_text: Listing, applications, messaging: everything is free. No credit card requested.
trust.p2_text: A coffee, a visit of the home, a walkthrough of instructions. Trust isn't guessed: it is built by looking at each other.
trust.p3_text: ID checked within 24 hours, reviews published by previous owners, history of completed sits. Everything is transparent.
trust.p4_title: From your street to the other end of France
trust.p4_text: Hyper-local for a quick fix, expanded for a change of scenery: you decide how far you want to go. Every sit becomes an opening door.

testimonials.title: They dared. Here is what stayed with them.
testimonials.source: Testimonials collected from Founder program members (January – May 2026). Real first names and cities, stories anonymized at their request.

story.title: It all started with a visa.
story.p1: The habit of opening up to locals has been lost. For lack of a pretext.
story.p2: Elisa, my partner and co-founder of Guardiens, arrived from Argentina with a visa that didn't allow her to work. She looked after animals. She came home with stories: people who opened their doors, their lives, freely. Strangers who ended up inviting us for Christmas.
story.p3: Elisa and I looked after 37 houses in five years, in villages, cities, and hamlets.
story.quote: We never just looked after houses. We were invited into lives.
story.p4: This is what the village used to do naturally.
story.p5: Guardiens is the pretext that was missing. On one side, small favours of mutual aid between local people: a vegetable garden watered in exchange for a meal, one skill for another, a favour returned in kind. On the other, independently, house-sitting: your keys entrusted to someone you've looked in the eye.
story.p6: You will leave with peace of mind. You will return with a story. We didn't expect it to matter so much.
story.p7: That is why we built Guardiens, so that you could live this too.

cities.title: House-sitting near you.
cities.lede: Practical guides to prepare for your sit, and local hubs for the most active cities, Lyon, Annecy, Grenoble, and all over France.
cities.cities_footer: Whatever your department, you will find a sitter nearby.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.a1: House-sitting is an exchange of services: a caretaker / pet & home sitter lives in your home at no cost while you are away and takes care of your pets. The exchange involves no financial transaction between the two parties.
faq.q2: Do I have to pay to register as an owner?
faq.a2: Registration, posting ads, and communicating with sitters are all available without requiring a subscription for owners. No credit card requested. Caretakers benefit from access without subscription until July 14, 2026, then the subscription is €6.99/month. Local mutual aid remains free of subscription for everyone.
faq.q3: How can I find a pet sitter near me?
faq.a3: Register on Guardiens, post your house-sitting ad with dates and animals, and receive applications from sitters living near you. You choose after a meeting.
faq.q4: Who are the caretakers on Guardiens?
faq.a4: Local people, verified by our team. Each profile undergoes an identity verification (ID + selfie) processed within 24 hours. You can also see their reviews published by previous owners.
faq.q5: How does a sit work on Guardiens?
faq.a5: You post your ad, local sitters apply, you choose after a meeting, then your sitter moves in. An optional house-sitting agreement outlines the commitments of each party during the stay.
faq.q6: Is Guardiens available everywhere in France ?
faq.a6: Yes. The Guardiens network extends to all regions of France, from the Basque Country to Brittany, via the Alps and the North. You will find a sitter near you regardless of your department.

final.title: Your next story begins here.
final.lede: You will leave with peace of mind. You will return with something else: a new place discovered, a meeting, someone you otherwise would never have crossed paths with.
final.footnote: Registration in 2 minutes, no credit card required.
